entries.title: Getting Started
entries.description: Start with installation.md. Pick the desktop app or the Python CLI. Follow first project.md if you want the desktop path from project creation to publish. Read concepts.md if you want the pipeline model before you edit c
headings: If you use the desktop app
keywords: If you use the desktop app

entries.title: CLI quickstart
entries.description: Drive Niamoto from a shell. For the desktop path, read first project.md.

entries.title: Installation
entries.description: Niamoto gives you two entry points: a desktop app and a Python CLI. Install the desktop app unless you need automation, CI, or server runs.

entries.title: Niamoto core concepts
entries.description: Niamoto runs one pipeline through three config files:

entries.title: Your first Niamoto project
entries.description: Follow the desktop app from the welcome screen to a published portal.

entries.title: Desktop App Tour
entries.description: Use this section when you already have the desktop app installed and want the full UI path from project setup to publication.

entries.title: Collections
entries.description: Collections is the reader facing name for the desktop area that manages grouped outputs. Behind the interface, this stage spans config/transform.yml and the collection facing parts of config/export.yml.
headings: How this stage fits the full flow
keywords: How this stage fits the full flow

entries.title: Import
entries.description: Import is where a project becomes usable. Add the source files, let Niamoto detect their roles, review the generated configuration, then load the data into the project workspace.

entries.title: Preview Surfaces
entries.description: Preview is not one separate stage of the product. It appears inside three different parts of the desktop workflow.
headings: 2. Site preview in the Site Builder
keywords: 2. Site preview in the Site Builder

entries.title: Publish
entries.description: Publish is the final desktop stage. Use it to build the generated site, inspect the result, choose a deployment target, and follow the deployment status to the end.
headings: 1. Build and inspect the generated site
keywords: 1. Build and inspect the generated site

entries.title: Site Builder
entries.description: The Site area turns imported and computed project content into the public portal structure. This is where you shape pages, navigation, and presentation before you move to Publish.

entries.title: Widget Catalogue and Collection Extras
entries.description: This page is a supporting reference for the Collections area. Use it when the main collections.md page is not enough and you need to focus on widget selection, list pages, or API oriented outputs.
headings: Collection extras beyond a single widget
keywords: Collection extras beyond a single widget

entries.title: CLI & Automation
entries.description: Run this path when you want the Niamoto pipeline from a shell, CI job, or server.
headings: Refresh a project from the shell
keywords: Refresh a project from the shell

entries.title: Plugin Development
entries.description: Build custom plugins for Niamoto without patching the core runtime.

entries.title: Building Widgets
entries.description: Widgets render HTML for html page exporter. Niamoto validates widget params, calls render(data, params), then wraps the result in the standard widget container.

entries.description: Here are some complete configuration examples in import.yml for different API scenarios:

entries.title: Custom Exporters
entries.description: Exporters turn validated export.yml targets into files, folders, or API payloads. ExporterService passes the whole target config to the plugin, not a loose data/config/output path triple.
headings: What Niamoto Passes To The Exporter
keywords: What Niamoto Passes To The Exporter

entries.title: Darwin Core Export Guide
entries.description: This guide covers exporting biodiversity data in Darwin Core format, the global standard for sharing biological occurrence information. Niamoto's Darwin Core export enables seamless integration with GBIF, iDigBio, and ot

entries.title: Database Aggregator
entries.description: database aggregator is a real transformer plugin. Niamoto ships it in src/niamoto/core/plugins/transformers/aggregation/database aggregator.py.

entries.description: Use database aggregator when you need read only SQL inside a transformer entry.

entries.title: Guide de développement de plugins
entries.description: Ce guide explique comment créer des plugins pour la plateforme Niamoto. Les plugins permettent d'étendre les fonctionnalités sans modifier le code principal.

entries.title: Anonymous Holdout Rework (2026 03 21)
entries.description: The previous anonymous holdout was synthetic, overly easy, and permanently inflated the benchmark. This rework replaced it with a holdout built from real gold set columns anonymized with generic names.

entries.title: Reference
entries.description: This section collects source level and configuration references for Niamoto. Use it when you need the current command surface, plugin contracts, YAML boundaries, database shape, or preview API.

entries.title: API Export Guide
entries.description: Niamoto can publish machine readable outputs alongside the website export. The implementation lives in the exporter layer.

entries.title: CLI Commands Reference
entries.description: The CLI itself is the source of truth for flags and subcommands. This page points to the main command groups and the docs that explain how they fit together.

entries.title: Configuration Analysis
entries.description: Use this checklist when reviewing a complex configuration change.

entries.title: Configuration Guide
entries.description: Niamoto splits configuration by pipeline stage so each file answers one question.

entries.title: Core API Reference
entries.description: Niamoto does not maintain a separate handwritten "core API" document. The practical entry points are the autogenerated package reference and the service, plugin, publish, and GUI API modules used by the product.

entries.title: Database Schema Reference
entries.description: DuckDB is the default project database at db/niamoto.duckdb. SQLite still works for .db and .sqlite paths, but new desktop projects default to DuckDB.

How to inspect a project database
keywords: Configuration files that shape the schema

entries.title: Preview API Reference
entries.description: The desktop backend renders HTML previews for widgets and related preview surfaces in the GUI.

entries.title: Templates and Hierarchy Patterns
entries.description: Reusable templates help when a block stays stable across several entities or widgets. Hierarchies stay easier to debug when identifiers and parent child relationships remain visible in configuration.
